project.title: LC-MS/MS data for HCA mRNA antibodies
project.projectDescription: Fc N-glycosylation of IgGs is necessary for effector functions and is a crucial component of quality control. Fc N-glycan profiles of HCA produced by two platforms were determined using mass spectrometry which revealed major differences in site occupancy, glycan types, and glycoform distribution.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: HCA produced by two platforms, namely Nicotiana-based manufacturing (HCA-N) and mRNA-mediated expression (HCAmrna) in vaginal cells. IgGs were digested with Trypsin followed by desalting with C18 ziptip prior to mass spectrometry analysis.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: After digested with Trypsin, the digestion was submitted to an Orbitrap Lumos mass spectrometer. The obtained raw data sets were processed by Byoinc software to analysis the glycosylation of the IgGs.

labPIs.title: Dr
labPIs.affiliation: Boston University Chobanian & Avedisian School of Medicine
labPIs.name: Catherine E. Costello

references.referenceLine: Nador E, Xia C, Santangelo PJ, Whaley KJ, Costello CE, Anderson DJ. Platform-Specific Fc N-Glycan Profiles of an Antisperm Antibody. Antibodies (Basel). 2024 13(1):17

Characteristics[CleavageAgent]: trypsin (C-terminal cleavage at arginine and lysine, 1:50, enzyme:protein)
Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: The HCA IgG protein was characterized by nano ultra-high-performance liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry (nanoUPLC-MS and MS/MS).
Comment[Instrument]: Orbitrap Fusion Lumos Tribrid mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Waltham, MA, USA)
ACQUITY UPLC M-Class system (Waters Corp., Milford, MA, USA)
Comment[IonizationType]: MS analyses were performed in the positive mode
Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance]: Mass tolerance was set to 10 and 20 ppm at the MS and MS/MS levels, respectively.
Comment[FragmentMassTolerance]: Mass tolerance was set to 10 and 20 ppm at the MS and MS/MS levels, respectively.
Comment[Separation]: nanoEase MZ HSS C18 T3 UPLC column (100 Å, 1.8 μm, 75 μm × 100 mm, Waters)
Comment[EnrichmentMethod]: deglycosylated with PNGase F (New England Biolabs Inc., Ipswich, MA, USA) at 37 °C for 16 h prior to MS analysis.
